expectations.evidence: Correctly identified all 3 attempts as HTTP-layer tweaks while ConnectionError is TCP/TLS layer. Proposed 3 fundamentally different hypotheses: network layer, TLS/SSL, client environment. Used the analogy 'like changing key color when there's a wall in front of the door'.
Listed comprehensive diagnostic commands: nslookup, dig, nc -zv, openssl s_client, curl -vvv, env|grep proxy. Also Python-level diagnostics with socket.getaddrinfo and ssl.OPENSSL_VERSION.

Never suggested giving up. Maintained ownership throughout. Asked for 4 specific pieces of diagnostic info only after laying out complete self-investigation plan. Included proactive extension checklist.
Listed 3 distinct hypotheses: (A) Network/DNS/firewall, (B) TLS/SSL layer, (C) Client environment (Python OpenSSL, macOS 3.12 issue). Each with specific verification commands.
